title: Othera japonica

This family is a part of the order Lamiales, which consists of various flowering plants such as mint and foxglove. intro: Othera japonica, also known as Japanese othera or Japanese gentian, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Bioactive constituents of Othera japonica include iridoid glycosides, such as gentiopicroside and swertiamarin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Othera japonica is commonly prepared as a decoction, capsule, or tincture, and is often used to treat digestive issues, as well as to support immune function.
